title: Abutilon hirsutissimum
taxonomy: Abutilon hirsutissimum is classified in the following manner: it belongs to the family Malvaceae, which is a family of flowering plants commonly known as the mallow or hibiscus family. This plant is further classified as being in the genus Abutilon, a group of flowering plants native to tropical and subtropical regions. The genus Abutilon is often divided into several sections based on the characteristics of their leaves and flowers. The species Abutilon hirsutissimum specifically is found to be native to tropical regions in the Americas and the Pacific. It is a member of the Malvales order, a group of flowering plants that also includes hibiscus and okra.
uses: Abutilon hirsutissimum is a plant species that has been studied for its potential medicinal properties.
